title: Rounding and chance
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: Four of the five numbers come out with a fractional part. Money, cups and bags do not come in pieces.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.no-round: You are not using rounding to the nearest.
why.no-floor: You are not using rounding down.
why.no-ceil: You are not using rounding up.
why.no-random: You are not using a random number.
hint2.no-random: The dice should not give the same thing every time.
hint2.wrong-rounded: 47 divided by 6 is 7.83. The nearest whole number is 8.
hint2.wrong-cups: 20 lev, at 3 a cup. That gives 6.67 — but there is nothing to pay for the seventh cup with.
hint3.wrong-cups: When the remainder is not enough, you round DOWN. The fractional part is simply cut off.
hint2.wrong-beans: 20 kilos of coffee, 3 to a bag. That gives 6.67 — but the remainder has to go somewhere too.
hint3.wrong-beans: When the remainder needs another whole one, you round UP. The same numbers, the opposite direction.
why.wrong-dice: The dice do not give a number from 1 to 6.
hint2.wrong-dice: The random number comes out between zero and one, with a fractional part. It has to be stretched and rounded.
hint3.wrong-dice: Multiplied by 6 it gives between 0 and almost 6. Rounded down it gives a whole number from 0 to 5. Plus one moves it to 1 through 6.
why.wrong-count: There are not five lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: A bill of 47 for six people. Twenty lev of coffee at three a cup. Twenty kilos of beans in bags of three. And some dice.
blocks.1.text: Every division comes out with a fractional part. But money is paid in whole units, cups are counted in whole units, and bags are bought in whole units. Nowhere can 6.67 remain.
blocks.3.text: Math holds the mathematical operations. Three of them turn a fractional number into a whole one, in different ways.
blocks.5.text: The first gives 8 — the nearest. The second gives 7 — always down. The third gives 8 — always up.
blocks.7.text: Down, when the remainder is not enough for another one. Six whole cups and sixty-seven stotinki that buy nothing.
blocks.8.text: Up, when the remainder has to fit somewhere. Six full bags and a few more beans that need a seventh bag.
blocks.10.text: Math.random gives a number between zero and one. Never exactly one. On its own it is useless — it has to be stretched to the range you want.
blocks.12.items.0: the random number is between 0 and almost 1
blocks.12.items.1: times 6 it becomes between 0 and almost 6
blocks.12.items.2: rounded down it gives 0 to 5, plus 1 moves it to 1 through 6
blocks.14.text: Fix the four lines. The first one, showing the exact division, stays as it is.
blocks.15.items.3: the dice give a whole number from 1 to 6
